what_it_checks: hermes binary is resolvable on PATH
failure_symptom: All tool/subprocess calls to the host fail immediately
likely_causes: Hermes not installed
PATH not exported in this shell/agent env
fix_steps: Run: which hermes
If missing, install/repair Hermes Agent and ensure its bin dir is on PATH
For agents, confirm the spawned shell inherits PATH
change_robust: Uses shutil.which at runtime — independent of any hardcoded path
detection_method: shutil.which(ctx.hermes_bin) is not None

what_it_checks: hermes --version is parseable (host is a known-good build)
failure_symptom: Unknown host version; contract assumptions may not hold
likely_causes: Hermes build does not print the expected banner
Broken install
fix_steps: Run: hermes --version
Reinstall Hermes if the version banner is missing
change_robust: Parses live --version output, not a pinned constant
detection_method: regex 'Hermes Agent v<ver>' matches `hermes --version`

what_it_checks: host advertises -z/--oneshot (the non-interactive entry every plugin relies on)
failure_symptom: Plugin/CLI falls into interactive mode or errors when invoking the host
likely_causes: A Hermes update renamed/removed the oneshot flag
fix_steps: Run: hermes --help | grep -- -z
If absent, pin to a compatible Hermes version or update the plugin to the new flag
change_robust: Greps live --help output each run — detects a Hermes update that breaks the contract
detection_method: '-z' and '--oneshot' present in `hermes --help`

what_it_checks: this plugin's entry point is registered under hermes_agent.plugins and import+register() works
failure_symptom: Plugin not loaded by Hermes; its tools/subcommand are absent
likely_causes: pip install incomplete
entry point metadata missing
register() import error
fix_steps: Run: python -c "import importlib.metadata as m; print([e.value for e in m.entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') if 'cluxion-agentplugin-autoclearmemory' in e.name.lower()])"
Reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-Agentplugin-AutoClearMemory
Confirm the entry module imports and exposes a callable register
change_robust: Reads live importlib.metadata.entry_points(), not a config file
detection_method: entry_points(group='hermes_agent.plugins') contains this dist and register is callable

what_it_checks: this plugin's toolset 'forgetforge' is listed and enabled by `hermes tools list`
failure_symptom: Model cannot see this plugin's tools; --toolsets silently ignores it
likely_causes: Plugin failed to register its toolset
Hermes toolset registration API changed
fix_steps: Run: hermes tools list | grep forgetforge
If missing, reinstall cluxion-Agentplugin-AutoClearMemory and restart the host
Verify register_tool used a valid toolset name
change_robust: Queries live `hermes tools list` for 'forgetforge' — catches contract drift on Hermes upgrade
detection_method: 'forgetforge' appears under plugin toolsets in `hermes tools list`

what_it_checks: installed distribution version equals the package __version__
failure_symptom: Stale or mismatched install; fixes/behaviors may not be the deployed ones
likely_causes: Partial upgrade
multiple installs shadowing each other
fix_steps: Run: pip show cluxion-Agentplugin-AutoClearMemory
Force a clean reinstall: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-Agentplugin-AutoClearMemory
change_robust: Compares live importlib.metadata.version() to __version__ — no hardcoding
detection_method: importlib.metadata.version('cluxion-Agentplugin-AutoClearMemory') == __version__

what_it_checks: native module forgetforge_engine_native imports (else pure-Python/subprocess fallback is used)
failure_symptom: Slower path; if fallback also broke, tool errors
likely_causes: wheel ABI/arch mismatch
native package not installed
fix_steps: Run: python -c 'import forgetforge_engine_native'
If ImportError, reinstall the bundled wheel; fallback keeps working meanwhile
change_robust: Attempts the import + hasattr at runtime; WARN (not fail) because fallback works
detection_method: import forgetforge_engine_native succeeds

what_it_checks: _wrap catches all exceptions (e.g. TypeError from float()) → error JSON, never raises to host
failure_symptom: Traceback escapes into the host when the model emits a wrong-typed number field
likely_causes: _wrap only caught (ValueError, OSError)
fix_steps: Broaden _wrap except to Exception
change_robust: Invokes a handler with a list where a number is expected and asserts error JSON
detection_method: handler raising TypeError yields {ok:false,...}

what_it_checks: SQLite database file exists at expected path (FORGETFORGE_HOME/db.sqlite) and can be opened
failure_symptom: FileNotFoundError, database is locked (sqlite3.OperationalError with 'locked'), or database format error (sqlite3.DatabaseError)
likely_causes: forgetforge init never ran (db not created)
FORGETFORGE_HOME environment variable points to non-existent or inaccessible directory
Disk full or filesystem read-only
Another process holds exclusive lock on db.sqlite (pruner daemon still running)
Corrupted SQLite file (truncated, overwritten, or partial write)
User lacks read/write permissions on ~/.forgetforge/ or db.sqlite
fix_steps: Initialize if missing: python -m forgetforge.cli init
Check FORGETFORGE_HOME: python -c "from forgetforge.config import default_home; print(default_home())"
Verify directory exists and is writable: ls -ld ~/.forgetforge && touch ~/.forgetforge/test && rm ~/.forgetforge/test
Check for locks: lsof ~/.forgetforge/db.sqlite (kill any holding process if stale)
Validate DB integrity: python -c "import sqlite3; conn=sqlite3.connect('$HOME/.forgetforge/db.sqlite'); conn.execute('PRAGMA integrity_check')" (expect 'ok')
If corrupted, backup and restore from archive: mv ~/.forgetforge/db.sqlite ~/.forgetforge/db.sqlite.backup && forgetforge init
change_robust: Probes actual file and DB state; detects corruption, locks, permissions at runtime before operations. Hermes updates do not affect this check.
detection_method: Construct db_path from load_config(); check Path.exists() and try sqlite3.connect(db_path, timeout=5.0)

what_it_checks: SQLite schema version is correct: memories table exists with all required columns (id, content, tier, importance, frequency, retrieval_count, is_procedural, keep_forever, forget_requested, created_at, updated_at, last_recall_at); retrieval_events table exists; FTS5 virtual table created
failure_symptom: sqlite3.OperationalError 'no such table' or 'no such column'; FTS5 query fails; migrations incomplete from earlier version
likely_causes: Database created by older plugin version (schema mismatch)
Manual schema modifications or deletions
Partial migration if plugin was interrupted during upgrade
db.sqlite file is wrong version from backup
Schema alter operations left table in inconsistent state
fix_steps: Check schema: python -c "import sqlite3; conn=sqlite3.connect('$HOME/.forgetforge/db.sqlite'); print([col[1] for col in conn.execute('PRAGMA table_info(memories)').fetchall()])"
Check FTS5 exists: python -c "import sqlite3; conn=sqlite3.connect('$HOME/.forgetforge/db.sqlite'); conn.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memories_fts')"
If schema missing, re-init cleanly: rm -rf ~/.forgetforge && forgetforge init
If only FTS5 missing, manually recreate: forgetforge prune (will recreate on demand during db.connect)
Backup old data first: sqlite3 ~/.forgetforge/db.sqlite '.dump memories' > memories_backup.sql
change_robust: Queries live schema introspection (PRAGMA table_info); detects schema drift at runtime. Checks after db.connect() to catch partial migrations.
detection_method: PRAGMA table_info(memories); SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memories_fts; compare column names and count to expected schema in db.py SCHEMA constant

what_it_checks: SQLite WAL (Write-Ahead Logging) journal mode is enabled for better concurrency
failure_symptom: PRAGMA journal_mode returns 'delete' or other mode; concurrent operations slow down or fail with 'database is locked'
likely_causes: Filesystem or database opened without PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL set (old code path)
Filesystem does not support WAL (some network mounts, old filesystems)
WAL mode disabled in config or by manual SQL
Forced backend without WAL support
fix_steps: Verify current mode: python -c "import sqlite3; conn=sqlite3.connect('$HOME/.forgetforge/db.sqlite'); print(conn.execute('PRAGMA journal_mode').fetchone()[0])"
Explicitly enable WAL: python -c "import sqlite3; conn=sqlite3.connect('$HOME/.forgetforge/db.sqlite'); conn.execute('PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL'); conn.close()"
If filesystem issue, check for .sqlite-wal and .sqlite-shm files: ls -la ~/.forgetforge/db.sqlite* (should exist if WAL active)
For network mounts, use alternative filesystem or local disk for FORGETFORGE_HOME
change_robust: Probes live PRAGMA setting; detects configuration drift. Re-enabled automatically on next db.connect() by SCHEMA pragma.
detection_method: PRAGMA journal_mode after connecting; expect 'wal' (case-insensitive)

what_it_checks: SQLite FTS5 full-text search extension is compiled in and working
failure_symptom: sqlite3.OperationalError 'no such module: fts5' when searching memories
likely_causes: SQLite compiled without FTS5 extension (uncommon, most systems have it)
Incorrect SQLite version on system
Custom SQLite binary without FTS5
fix_steps: Check FTS5 support: python -c "import sqlite3; conn=sqlite3.connect(':memory:'); conn.execute('CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE t USING fts5(c)')" (no error = OK)
If missing, upgrade or rebuild Python (includes SQLite): pip install --upgrade python or use system package manager
Fallback to LIKE search is automatic (check db.search_memories fallback path)
change_robust: Tests FTS5 availability in live database; automatically handles missing FTS5 with LIKE fallback (slower but functional). Checked on each search operation.
detection_method: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE test_fts USING fts5(content); drop it after success

what_it_checks: config.yaml at FORGETFORGE_HOME/config.yaml exists and parses correctly as valid YAML; pruner and threshold sections are dicts if present
failure_symptom: FileNotFoundError, yaml.YAMLError, yaml.scanner.ScannerError; values are None or wrong type; defaults not applied
likely_causes: config.yaml corrupted or syntax error (missing colons, bad indentation)
Non-mapping values in pruner or thresholds sections (list, scalar, null)
Non-numeric values in interval_hours or thresholds
config.yaml does not exist (uses defaults—not always bad)
Wrong HOME directory due to FORGETFORGE_HOME misconfiguration
fix_steps: Check YAML syntax: python -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('$HOME/.forgetforge/config.yaml'))"
Inspect values: python -c "from forgetforge.config import load_config; cfg=load_config(); print(f'interval_hours={cfg.pruner_interval_hours}, cold_threshold={cfg.cold_retention_threshold}')"
Restore from example: cp $PLUGIN_ROOT/config.yaml.example ~/.forgetforge/config.yaml
Or delete to use defaults: rm ~/.forgetforge/config.yaml (reinit will create it)
Validate all numeric values are 0-360 (hours), 0-1.0 (thresholds)
change_robust: Parses live YAML and validates ForgetForgeConfig type; detects malformed config at load time. Defaults gracefully if file missing.
detection_method: load_config() succeeds; inspect loaded ForgetForgeConfig dataclass fields are all valid types (int, float, Path)

what_it_checks: FORGETFORGE_HOME environment variable (if set) points to a valid, writable directory; if unset, defaults to ~/.forgetforge
failure_symptom: FileNotFoundError, PermissionError, or OSError when trying to mkdir(parents=True)
likely_causes: FORGETFORGE_HOME set to non-existent parent directory without write access
Path contains invalid characters or is too long
Home directory inaccessible (NFS mount down, permissions changed)
Disk full
User lost permissions on ~/.forgetforge after initial setup
fix_steps: Check FORGETFORGE_HOME: echo $FORGETFORGE_HOME
Verify it exists and is writable: mkdir -p "$FORGETFORGE_HOME" && touch "$FORGETFORGE_HOME/test" && rm "$FORGETFORGE_HOME/test"
If not set, check default: unset FORGETFORGE_HOME && python -c "from forgetforge.config import default_home; print(default_home())"
Fix permissions if needed: chmod 700 ~/.forgetforge
If using custom path in FORGETFORGE_HOME, ensure parent exists: mkdir -p $(dirname "$FORGETFORGE_HOME")
change_robust: Probes default_home() and expanduser() at runtime; validates directory creation. Checks before any DB operation.
detection_method: Call default_home() from config.py; check Path.expanduser() handles ~; attempt to create subdirectory in target

what_it_checks: PyArrow library is installed for Parquet archive format; graceful fallback to JSONL if missing
failure_symptom: Archive format is 'jsonl' (fallback), not 'parquet'; no .parquet files written; memory usage higher when archiving many cold memories
likely_causes: PyArrow not installed (optional dependency)
PyArrow version incompatible with installed NumPy
system libarrow library missing (Linux/Mac ARM)
fix_steps: Install PyArrow: pip install pyarrow>=15.0
Verify: python -c "import pyarrow; print(pyarrow.__version__)"
If JSONL fallback is acceptable, no action required (archiving still works, just slower)
For better compression, ensure PyArrow available before large pruner runs
change_robust: Detects PyArrow at import time; archive operations fall back gracefully to JSONL format. Check performed during first archive operation.
detection_method: import pyarrow; import pyarrow.parquet; call archive.write_cold_archive_batch() and check returned format field

what_it_checks: Hermes plugin hook 'pre_llm_call' is registered (if running inside Hermes); hot context builder is callable
failure_symptom: hot_context_payload() returns empty dict or raises error; hot memories not appearing in LLM context; hook not firing on pre_llm_call
likely_causes: Plugin not loaded in Hermes (not in enabled plugins list in config.yaml)
register_hook not available in Hermes context (running outside Hermes)
Database error in hot_context builder prevents hook from executing
Hook registration fails due to typo or incompatible Hermes version
fix_steps: Verify plugin enabled in Hermes: grep -A5 'plugins:' ~/.hermes/config.yaml | grep 'cluxion-agentplugin-autoclearmemory'
Test hook manually: python -c "from forgetforge.adapters.hermes import _pre_llm_hot_inject; print(_pre_llm_hot_inject())"
Check Hermes logs: tail -f ~/.hermes/logs/*.log | grep -i forgetforge
Restart Hermes after enabling plugin: hermes stop && sleep 2 && hermes start
If Hermes has no register_hook method, plugin gracefully degrades (hook not registered, but tools still work)
change_robust: Probes hook registration at startup (detects if Hermes context available); hot_context_payload() can be called independently to test hook logic.
detection_method: Call hot_inject.hot_context_payload(); inspect hermes.adapters.register() for register_hook presence; check Hermes logging for hook registration

what_it_checks: All 7 Hermes tool schemas (forgetforge_store, recall, keep, forget, status, import_brief, hot_context) are valid JSON and have required 'name', 'description', 'parameters' keys
failure_symptom: KeyError when accessing schema[key], json.JSONDecodeError, or schema validation fails in Hermes registry (tools don't appear in model list)
likely_causes: schemas.py corrupted or has syntax error
Tool names don't match handler function signatures in hermes.py
Required parameter keys missing (e.g., 'type': 'object')
Enum values in schema don't match handler code (e.g., 'layer' enum vs actual layers)
fix_steps: Validate schema JSON: python -c "from forgetforge.schemas import *; import json; [json.dumps(s) for s in [RECALL_SCHEMA, STORE_SCHEMA, KEEP_SCHEMA, FORGET_SCHEMA, STATUS_SCHEMA, IMPORT_BRIEF_SCHEMA, HOT_CONTEXT_SCHEMA]]"
Check all tool names match: grep 'name.*forgetforge' src/forgetforge/schemas.py | wc -l (expect 7)
Verify enum values match code: grep -o '"enum": \[.*\]' src/forgetforge/schemas.py and cross-check with handlers
Re-install plugin: pip install --force-reinstall cluxion-Agentplugin-AutoClearMemory
Restart Hermes to re-register tools
change_robust: Validates schema structure at import time; JSON serialization ensures correctness before Hermes receives it. Schemas are static, checked on plugin load.
detection_method: Import schemas module; validate each schema dict has required keys; attempt json.dumps() on each; call json.loads() to round-trip

what_it_checks: If pruner daemon is running (forgetforge pruner-daemon), it is alive, responsive, and not consuming excessive CPU/memory
failure_symptom: Daemon process exists but is defunct (zombie), uses 100% CPU, or consumes gigabytes of memory; logs show repeated errors
likely_causes: Parent shell exited without waiting for daemon (daemon became orphan, reparented to init)
Infinite loop in pruner.run_pruner() due to OOM or bad memory state
Database lock preventing pruner from completing, causing retry loop
Unhandled exception in pruner causes hang
Daemon started multiple times (multiple processes)
fix_steps: List all forgetforge processes: ps aux | grep forgetforge
Kill any stale daemon: pkill -9 'forgetforge.*pruner-daemon'
Check Hermes startup script doesn't auto-start daemon: grep -r 'pruner-daemon' ~/.hermes/
If daemon needed, run with a bounded cycle cap under a service manager: forgetforge pruner-daemon --max-cycles 24
Monitor logs: tail -f ~/.forgetforge/pruner.log
Check for repeated 'database is locked': if present, stop other access and retry
change_robust: Process check via ps; daemon is optional (pruner can run on demand). Detects zombies and resource leaks at runtime.
detection_method: ps aux | grep 'forgetforge pruner-daemon' | grep -v grep; if found, test with kill -0 (check process exists); monitor RSS memory growth; log tail for errors

what_it_checks: Memories can reach 'hot' tier through normal recall operations; hot tier promotion is working (retrieval_count + layer boost + time decay)
failure_symptom: Memory stuck in 'cold' or 'warm_episodic' tier after recall; hot_context always empty even after recalls; hot tier memories never injected
likely_causes: days_since_recall calculation broken (returns NaN or inf)
Rust engine returns wrong tier decision despite valid inputs
Python fallback tier logic has bug (e.g., hot condition never true)
Retrieval boost calculation wrong (boost not applied or inverted)
Hot window days threshold misconfigured (set to 0 or very large)
fix_steps: Test manually: python -c "from forgetforge import db, store, recall; from forgetforge.config import load_config; cfg=load_config(); conn=db.connect(cfg.db_path); store.store_memory(conn, memory_id='test', content='x', importance=0.7); results=recall.recall_query(conn, 'test'); print(f\"Tier: {db.get_memory(conn, 'test').tier}\"); conn.close()"
Check days_since_recall: python -c "from forgetforge.recall import days_since; import datetime; print(f\"days_since(now)={days_since(datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat())}\")"
Verify hot window config: python -c "from forgetforge.config import load_config; print(load_config().hot_window_days)"
Force Python backend to test fallback: FORGETFORGE_ENGINE_BACKEND=python forgetforge recall test
Check rust_bridge._python_tier() logic directly with test cases
change_robust: Probes live recall and tier promotion; tests actual data path end-to-end. Detects logic bugs and config issues at runtime.
detection_method: Store a memory, call recall_query(), check resulting memory.tier is 'hot' or 'warm_episodic'; inspect decision logic in recall.py and rust_bridge._python_tier()

what_it_checks: Memory IDs are validated before storage: non-empty, unique, safe from SQL injection; duplicates handled correctly (upsert)
failure_symptom: Empty memory_id crashes or creates NULL entries; SQL injection succeeds (unlikely with parameterized queries); duplicate IDs not updated, old value persists
likely_causes: memory_id validation missing in store_memory (ValueError not raised for empty)
Memory ID not validated at db.upsert_memory level either
INSERT ... ON CONFLICT ... UPDATE logic doesn't properly update all fields
FTS upsert creates duplicates due to missing delete-first
fix_steps: Test empty ID: python -c "from forgetforge import store; store.store_memory(None, memory_id='', content='test')" (expect ValueError)
Test upsert: store same ID twice with different content, verify second call updates (not duplicate created)
Test special chars: store.store_memory(None, memory_id="id\x00null", content='test') (should sanitize or fail safely)
Verify parameterized queries: grep -n '?' src/forgetforge/db.py | head -20 (all placeholders, no string concat)
Check FTS upsert transaction: db._fts_upsert should DELETE before INSERT in single transaction
change_robust: Tests input validation at storage layer; detects SQLi via parameterized query inspection. Upsert semantics verified by comparing before/after snapshots.
detection_method: Call store.store_memory() with edge cases: empty '', special chars, very long (500+ chars); verify no SQLi in search; check upsert semantics (UPDATE on conflict)

what_it_checks: Retrieval counts and importance/frequency scores don't overflow or become negative; clamped to valid ranges [0.0, 1.0] for importance/frequency
failure_symptom: Retrieval count becomes very large (1e6+) causing overflow in scoring; importance/frequency outside [0,1] causing undefined behavior in tier decisions; NaN in retention score
likely_causes: No clamping in store.store_memory (importance/frequency accept any float)
Retrieval boost applied without saturation check (unbounded growth)
Rust engine doesn't handle NaN/inf gracefully
Python fallback scoring doesn't check max(0.0, ...) correctly
fix_steps: Check store_memory clamping: grep -A2 'max(0.0, min(1.0' src/forgetforge/store.py (should clamp importance/frequency)

Test extreme values: python -c "from forgetforge import rust_bridge; result=rust_bridge.decide_tier(days_since_recall=1e6, retrieval_count=1e10, importance=100.0, frequency=-50.0); print(result)" (expect valid tier, not NaN)
Verify Python fallback: FORGETFORGE_ENGINE_BACKEND=python ... (same test)
Check retrieval_count update logic: grep -B5 -A5 'retrieval_count =' src/forgetforge/recall.py (should accumulate, not reset)
change_robust: Tests with live API calls; extreme values probe boundary conditions. Detected during normal operation when very old or frequently accessed memories exist.
detection_method: Update memory with extreme values: retrieval_count=1e6, importance=100.0, frequency=-50.0; check stored values are clamped; verify no NaN/inf in scoring

what_it_checks: Contradiction detection (if enabled) runs without blocking memory storage; detected contradictions are warnings, not errors
failure_symptom: store_memory hangs or raises exception on contradiction detection; warnings list malformed or missing; detection always returns empty (disabled)
likely_causes: contradiction.detect_contradictions() has infinite loop or blocks on DB lock
Warning structure doesn't match expectation (missing 'contradiction_type', 'details')
Exception in detection not caught, bubbles up
FTS search within detect_contradictions fails silently
fix_steps: Test with contradictions: python -c "from forgetforge import store, db; from forgetforge.config import load_config; cfg=load_config(); conn=db.connect(cfg.db_path); store.store_memory(conn, memory_id='a', content='X is 1'); store.store_memory(conn, memory_id='b', content='X is 2', check_contradictions=True); conn.close()"
Disable if problematic: set check_contradictions=False in hermes adapter handlers (or add flag to env var)
Verify exception handling in detect_contradictions: grep -A5 'except' src/forgetforge/contradiction.py
change_robust: Contradiction detection is best-effort (optional); storage proceeds even if detection fails. Checked during store operations with catch-all exception handler.
detection_method: Call store_memory with check_contradictions=True; store conflicting memories; inspect returned warnings list

what_it_checks: CLI commands (forgetforge check, init, store, recall, etc.) return correct exit codes: 0 on success, 1 on not-found/validation error, 2 on usage error
failure_symptom: Commands return 0 even on error (success masked); exit code 2 on data validation error (wrong semantics); error message to stdout instead of stderr
likely_causes: cli.py handlers don't check return values or raise exceptions properly
print() to stdout instead of sys.stderr for errors
Missing 'if not ok: return 1' checks in handlers
argparse error doesn't exit 2 (customization broke defaults)
fix_steps: Test invalid memory_id: forgetforge keep nonexistent (expect exit 1)
Test no args: forgetforge (expect exit 2 + help to stderr)
Test store success: forgetforge store test --content 'x' (expect exit 0)
Test recall empty: forgetforge recall nonexistent (expect exit 0 + 'no_memories_matched')
Verify stderr: forgetforge 2>&1 | head -5 (should show usage/error)
change_robust: Exit codes are part of CLI contract; tested independently of Hermes. Check after each plugin update.
detection_method: Run each CLI command with valid and invalid args; capture exit code; verify error messages to stderr
